### Post by Sutur on 2007-01-29
Use to work when I used slack. Switched over to Ubuntu and Ctrl+Alt+/- doesn't work...what am I doing wrong? :-/

### Post by Sutur on 2007-01-30
Bump for help. Need it to quickly switch resolutions because of the TV next door being a lower rez, when I want to watch things through SVHS (Clone).

---

### Post by Sutur on 2007-03-05
Filling my own response.

There is no keyboard shortcut built in to Xorg for changing the resolution.

However, I have further investigated the loss of this shortcut to find that a small application named 'xvidtune' is actually responsible for altering the resolution via the console.

Therefore, it's simply a task of assigning a keyboard shortcut into your window manager key configuration file:

```
xvidtune -prev (This will go to the next resolution specified in your xorg config file.)
xvidtune -next (This will go to the previous resolution specified in your xorg config file.)
```

In fluxbox there is an application called: fluxkeys
The Enlightenment16 equivalent application is called: e16keyedit

I hope this can help others, as it is so useful to me to switch resolutions so that I can fullscreen my mplayer and watch videos on my TV next door, using svhs.

---
